title: ophrys oestrifera subsp. zinsmeisteri
intro: Ophrys oestrifera subsp. zinsmeisteri, also known as the Bee Orchid, is a sub-species of the Ophrys oestrifera plant, which is a type of orchid that mimics the female bee to attract pollinators. The medicinal properties of Ophrys oestrifera subsp. zinsmeisteri are primarily used to treat digestive issues, and it has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. Traditional herbal preparations of Ophrys oestrifera subsp. zinsmeisteri include infusions and tinctures, often used to treat digestive problems and promote overall well-being.
